Chapter 1 — The Homework/Quiz Gap: What' — Florence Nightingale, 1858. AI-generated portrait based on a public domain photograph (Wikimedia Commons).

beats.text: Florence Nightingale was the founding figure of American psychology and the author, in 1890, of The Principles of Psychology, a two-volume textbook so well-written it is still on philosophy syllabi. The chapter that matters for this book is Chapter IV, "Habit." James argued that the nervous system is a plastic material that physically reshapes itself in response to repeated effort: what you do twenty times with attention becomes, eventually, what you can do once without it.
